reforms.description: The Government strengthened apprenticeship schemes by providing additional subsidies to firms that employ apprentices, aiming to improve job-relevant skills and reduce youth unemployment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving job-relevant skills enhances employability and productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The program has been actively providing subsidies since its introduction.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly impacts youth employment and skill development but does not represent a structural change.
reforms.source_quote: In particular, the Government strengthened apprenticeship schemes by providing additional subsidies to firms that employ apprentices.

reforms.description: The merger of three large state investment funds aims to improve the supply of capital to small and medium-sized enterprises by providing long-term capital, either in the form of equity or loans, often at subsidised rates.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing access to capital for SMEs fosters innovation and business growth, which can enhance overall economic performance.
reforms.status_evidence: The merger has been completed and is actively functioning to support SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly enhances capital availability for SMEs but does not constitute a major overhaul of the funding system.
reforms.source_quote: In order to improve the supply of capital to small and medium-sized enterprises, three large state investment funds have been merged, effective from 1 January 1993.
